hero.title: GTM Summary
hero.description: Your commercial-and-delivery lens across VOIS — enriched Salesforce pipeline alongside the cross-functional cuts that matter most: recognised revenue vs sold deals, top-10 delivery risks on major deals, service-offering roadmap per tower, share-of-wallet against competitors, consolidated customer story for meetings, and AI-generated competitive briefs for major deals.

links.revenueRecognition: €124M gap | -6.2% projection vs actual · concentrated in 3 deals
links.deliveryRisk: 3 Red · 4 Amber | €289M of revenue under amber/red RAG
links.hrSignal: 612 open VRFs | Project Atlas hiring against 1,000-hire commitment

deals.milestones: 2 missed · Q3 cutover · UAT sign-off slip
deals.risks: Onshore skill gap · vendor handover risk

deliveryRisk.footerText: Top 10 total value: €590M · 3 Red (€256M) · 4 Amber (€241M) · 3 Green (€93M)

offerings.name: Sovereign Cloud EU
offerings.desc: Data-sovereign hosting · EU residency · scope-3 reporting

offerings.name: Industry LLMs
offerings.desc: Pre-tuned vertical models · telco / retail / fin-svcs

problems.name: EU billing-batch latency
problems.desc: Open 9 days · workaround in place · scoped fix Q2 · 2nd recurrence

problems.name: Service-now backlog > 7d
problems.desc: 142 tickets in backlog · Tier-3 capacity gap · staffing in flight

problems.name: Tier-3 onshore staffing
problems.desc: 12 critical roles open · MMD engagement lead flagged 25-Feb

problems.name: Cloud region outage — DE-West
problems.desc: 8-Feb · 47 min impact · RCA shared · credit applied

customerPerformance.synthesis: DT Group remains a strong promoter (NPS +48, ▲ 4 vs prior month) with positive trajectory over 12 months. Service health is rated green overall and engagement-lead sentiment is positive. Pressure points to acknowledge in meeting: recurring EU billing-batch latency (open 9 days, 2nd recurrence) and a Tier-3 onshore staffing gap which is feeding the Service-Now backlog. Talking points: AI Triage Bot rollout (Q2-26 GA) directly addresses Tier-3 backlog; Sovereign Cloud EU offer aligns with their data-residency programme; €28M edge-expansion opportunity is ready to position.

compA.name: Competitor A
signals: Revenue −4.2% YoY · soft commercial print, margin pressure cited by CFO.
Lost deal at AT-mid: VOIS-style Hire-to-Serve win publicly reported.
Supply chain · 90-day shipment delay on edge hardware confirmed in earnings.
Strategy shift · de-emphasising mid-market — opportunity to pursue IDEA Group accounts.

compB.name: Competitor B
signals: Revenue +9.1% YoY · cloud-services growth and AI services traction.
Major win · UK public sector framework (£180M / 5y) — credibility boost.
New offer · Industry-LLM bundle launched — direct overlap with our AI Co-Pilot Pack.
Hiring · 600+ AI roles posted — capacity-building signal.

customer.name: DT Group
signals: Steady results · revenue +1.1% · CapEx programme unchanged.
Strategy · public commitment to data-residency programme — Sovereign Cloud EU pitch.
Leadership · new CIO appointed Q1 · published comments on AI-Ops.
Procurement · multi-vendor framework refresh expected H2 — door for share-shift.

aiSummary.title: GTM Executive Summary
aiSummary.overall: Commercial performance is strong on the front-end with material delivery exposure. Pipeline and win-rate are tracking ahead of plan, but recognised revenue is 6.2% behind sold projection — concentrated in three large deals — and the top-10 deal portfolio carries 3 Red / 4 Amber RAGs covering €497M.

takeaways.text: Bookings: 7 new logos / Gen-2 deals booked in Mar-26.
Customer health: NPS strong on top-10 customers; DT Group at +48 (▲ 4).

watchpoints.text: Revenue recognition gap: −€124M (−6.2%) — Helios (−24%) and Orion (−38%) the largest variances.
Delivery RAG: 3 Red / 4 Amber in top 10 — €497M revenue under risk.
Share of Wallet: data still RED — IDEA at 21% is the clearest growth opportunity (€48M).

actions.text: Convene Helios & Orion delivery reviews this week — bring forward SME risk inputs.
Position Sovereign Cloud EU + Edge expansion ahead of DT Group meeting (28 Mar).
Confirm competitor list and refresh cadence for Share of Wallet view.

GTM.1.title: GTM.1 — Pipeline & Salesforce Feed
GTM.1.description: Enriched Salesforce pipeline view on the GTM persona dashboard, with the cross-functional cuts most relevant to Chris (revenue recognition, delivery risk on major deals) surfaced alongside.
GTM.1.acceptanceCriteria: Chris's persona view shows the pipeline plus the linked finance and delivery-risk signals on one screen, with a filter to top deals.
GTM.1.userStory: As the GTM Director, I want an enriched Salesforce pipeline view on my persona dashboard with the most relevant cross-functional cuts (revenue recognition, delivery risk on major deals) surfaced alongside, so that I see commercial performance and the linked downstream risks on one screen.
feedback: Surfaces pipeline and downstream metrics in one dashboard widget
Enriched with VRF recruiting requests metrics

GTM.1.1.title: GTM.1.1 — Revenue Recognition vs Targets
GTM.1.1.description: Closed-loop view linking sold deals to recognised revenue over the contract life, so the gap between deal projection and actual revenue is visible by deal and in aggregate.
GTM.1.1.acceptanceCriteria: Each major sold deal shows projected vs actual revenue over time with variance flagged. Aggregate view rolls these up by tower and customer.
GTM.1.1.userStory: As the GTM Director, I want a closed-loop view linking sold deals to recognised revenue over the contract life by deal and in aggregate, so that I can see and explain the gap between deal projection and actual revenue delivered.
feedback: Variance displayed clearly for Top 5 deals
Aggregate views roll up to Sarah's forecast inputs

GTM.1.2.title: GTM.1.2 — ★ Delivery Risk per Major Deal
GTM.1.2.description: Top-10 major-deal delivery-risk view combining SME-led risk inputs (quarterly population) with weekly RAG comments from delivery leads during the contract period.
GTM.1.2.acceptanceCriteria: User sees the top 10 deals (above the revenue threshold) with current RAG, missed milestones, current issues and the SME-identified risks for each, refreshed quarterly with SME inputs.
GTM.1.2.userStory: As the GTM Director, I want a top-10 major-deal delivery-risk view combining quarterly SME-led risk inputs with weekly RAG comments from delivery leads, so that pre- and post-contract delivery risks on the biggest deals are visible alongside the commercial view.
feedback: Integrates weekly status with quarterly review cycle data
SME flags focus on engineering and capability roadblocks

GTM.2.title: GTM.2 — Service Offering Roadmap
GTM.2.description: Visibility on new offerings and improvements to existing services across the service towers in the Hub.
GTM.2.acceptanceCriteria: GTM user sees per-tower a list of new and evolving service offerings with milestone status, so they know what is coming and can position it with customers.
GTM.2.userStory: As the GTM Director, I want per-tower visibility of new and evolving service offerings with milestone status sourced from Horizon 2, so that I know what is coming from each service tower and can position it confidently with customers.
feedback: Service lines represented are BVOIS, D&IT, CARE, I/O
Status fields map to Beta, Live, and Plan states

GTM.3.title: GTM.3 — Share of Wallet / Market Opportunity
GTM.3.description: View of the share-of-wallet with external competitors to track new opportunities that will increase market share for VOIS.
GTM.3.acceptanceCriteria: GTM user is able to compare the VOIS share of wallet against its competitors in the VOIS penetration dashboard.
GTM.3.userStory: As the GTM Director, I want a view of VOIS share-of-wallet versus external competitors and an indication of new opportunities to grow market share, so that I can identify upsell opportunities and target growth more effectively.
feedback: Competitor list details are placeholder data to be verified
Opportunity sizing connects to account manager pipelines

GTM.4.title: GTM.4 — Customer Satisfaction & Delivery Risks
GTM.4.description: Consolidated customer-performance page combining NPS with the MMD problem jobs and service-impacting incidents into one customer-facing story, ready for use in customer meetings.
GTM.4.acceptanceCriteria: User selects a customer and sees NPS trend, current open problem jobs, recent service-impacting incidents and the related narrative on one page.
GTM.4.userStory: As the GTM Director, I want a consolidated customer-performance page combining NPS, MMD problem jobs and service-impacting incidents into one story, so that I can walk into customer-facing meetings with a complete picture of satisfaction and delivery health.
feedback: DT Group is loaded as the default customer story
AI summary integrates metrics from incidents and feedback cycles

GTM.5.title: GTM.5 — Competitive Positioning
GTM.5.description: Aggregated competitor-positioning view drawn from public sources (company reports, market data, paid aggregators). AI-summary for sales teams ahead of major customer meetings (quarter up / down, supply chain, competitor deal-wins) to gain competitive advantage in deal negotiations.
GTM.5.acceptanceCriteria: Before a major customer meeting, the sales lead can pull a one-page AI-generated competitor / customer brief from the Hub covering recent public data points.
GTM.5.userStory: As the GTM Director, I want an aggregated competitor-positioning view drawn from public sources with AI-generated competitor / customer briefs ahead of major customer meetings, so that I can take a competitive advantage into deal negotiations.
feedback: 1-pager download is supported as a mock action trigger
Saves briefs on demand per selected meeting calendar
